title: Leaderboard Playground
description: Mod ideas to extend this template. Each one demonstrates a different layer of the Polkadot stack and reuses the existing game / scoreboard / contract separation.

quests.title: Swap the game
quests.summary: Replace Flappy Bird with your own game. Build a component that accepts GameComponentProps and calls onGameEnd(score) once when the game ends. The scoreboard, contract, leaderboard UI, and player input all keep working without changes — App.tsx is the only file you need to edit.
acceptance: A new game component lives under src/games/<your-game>/
Swapping it in App.tsx is a one-line change

quests.title: Bind scores to accounts
quests.summary: Replace the display-name identity with caller() (the H160 of the transaction signer). The contract maps Address → best score, and the leaderboard renders the address with a user-chosen display name stored separately. Score spoofing becomes impossible.
teaches: Revive H160 caller() and Address mapping
acceptance: Contract storage uses Address as the key for best score
Submissions can only update the caller's own score
Leaderboard renders both display name and short address

quests.title: Replace //Alice with a real signer
quests.summary: By default every submission is signed by the //Alice dev key. Wire up a real signer — the Polkadot extension via @polkadot-apps/signer, the playground.dot mobile session key, or a per-browser keypair stored in localStorage.
acceptance: User connects an account before submitting
Submissions are signed by the connected account
App.tsx surfaces the connected address in the UI

quests.title: Persist match history on Bulletin
quests.summary: Each match's full history (rounds, timestamps, replay data) gets uploaded to Bulletin Chain on game end. The contract stores only (player, score, latest_cid) — heavy data lives off-chain, content-addressed.
teaches: Splitting hot index from cold replay data
acceptance: Each submitted score is paired with a Bulletin CID
Clicking a leaderboard entry fetches and replays the match

quests.title: Cross-game scoring
quests.summary: Add a game_id field to ScoreEntry, ScoreboardAPI, and the contract so multiple games can share one leaderboard. Players accumulate points across all games; per-game leaderboards are still available via filter.
teaches: API evolution without breaking existing games
acceptance: Two distinct games write to the same contract
A 'global' tab sums points across games per player
